title: Antennaria aretioides
taxonomy: Antennaria aretioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America and is commonly known by several names, including mountain silverweed, western silverweed, and western everlasting. The classification of Antennaria aretioides places it within the genus Antennaria, which contains approximately 30 species of flowering plants. Antennaria aretioides is further classified as a member of the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flowering structure.

intro: Antennaria aretioides, also known as Wartwort or Mountain Everlasting, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and woolly leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Antennaria aretioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Antennaria aretioides is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments for topical application.
